tasks.description: Orchestrate scope, universe, and execution strategy for Beverages industry analysis. Define the exact analytical scope (Consumer Staples > Beverages), confirm time horizon (6-18 months), enumerate eligible tickers, and set up an execution approach that maximizes parallel data collection while enforcing the no-hallucination rule. Establish structures for evidence tracking and success criteria (≥3 high-conviction longs and ≥3 high-conviction shorts).

subtasks.description: Confirm industry scope as Beverages within Consumer Staples. Outline sub-industries (e.g., soft drinks, brewers, distillers & vintners) and note any geography or market-cap focus. Capture the 6–18 month horizon and value-first, fundamentals-driven strategy constraints.

subtasks.description: Define data schemas and working tables for each ticker: fundamentals (IS/BS/CF), valuation metrics (EV/EBIT, EV/FCF, FCF yield), factors, returns, qualitative notes (moat, pricing power, drivers, risks, catalysts), and evidence links.
Plan parallelization for heavy data-fetching steps. Determine which fetches can run concurrently without dependency conflicts (per-ticker fundamentals, returns, news/transcripts).

subtasks.description: Set evidence tracking approach: for each material claim, save at least one tool-derived datum (and link) using evidence tools. Define acceptance criteria for idea readiness and a fallback if key data are missing.

completion_evidence: Tool get_eligible_tickers returned list with 14 items

Established working data schema for each beverages ticker as follows: 1) Fundamentals – Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, key ratios, and analyst estimates. 2) Valuation metrics – EV, EBIT (trailing/forward), EV/EBIT, Free Cash Flow (FCF), FCF yield, EV/FCF. 3) Factor exposures – growth, value, momentum, quality, volatility (tool-computed). 4) Weekly returns (last year). 5) Qualitative notes: business moat, pricing power, key drivers, risks, near-term catalysts, capital allocation, and red/green flags. 6) Traceable evidence links per claim/tool. Structure enables systematic data collection and analysis per plan, with flexibility for sub-industry/region tagging.

Every investment thesis bullet/claim will be anchored to at least one item of tool-derived evidence (financials, factor scores, transcripts, news, valuation metrics, or industry/sub-industry benchmarks). No assertion will be made without tool-based support. If key valuation, margin, or business quality data is missing for a ticker, conviction will be reduced or the idea skipped. Acceptance: evidence for moat, margins, FCF, leverage, valuation, catalysts, and risks must be present for all recommendations, with explicit notation of any critical gaps.

tasks.description: Construct industry and sub-industry benchmarks to anchor relative assessment. Use industry and sub-industry tools to compute reference medians/IQRs for factors and key valuation metrics; map each ticker to its appropriate peer set.

subtasks.description: Pull Beverages industry benchmark calculations across factors (growth, value, momentum, quality, volatility) to understand sector distributions.

Applied long selection filter to all eligible beverage tickers with sufficient data: MNST (Monster), CCEP (Coca-Cola Europacific Partners), PRMB (Primo Brands), TAP (Molson Coors), and SAM (Boston Beer). All display durable moats (category or regional leadership), stable-to-expanding margins (gross margin accretion or recovery), high/improving FCF, and either low or manageable leverage. Factor and transcript review confirms momentum and/or successful execution against cost or distribution initiatives. Each is supported by tool-derived evidence from recent fundamentals and qualitative disclosures.

tasks.description: Run scenario analysis and finalize conviction. Stress-test key assumptions (pricing, volume, margins, FX, input costs) under conservative/base/upside cases. Define invalidation triggers and adjust conviction levels accordingly.

subtasks.description: Build simple sensitivities for EV/EBIT and FCF yield under margin and volume shifts; quantify impact using calculator per ticker.
Define clear invalidation triggers (e.g., gross margin compression >X bps despite price increases; volume declines continue despite promo) tailored to each thesis.
Translate scenario outcomes and triggers into final conviction scores and recommended sizing bias (qualitative) per idea.
Record scenario outputs and final conviction as evidence supporting recommendations.

tasks.description: Format and deliver final recommendations. Compile outputs strictly to the required schema for each recommended ticker (≥3 Longs and ≥3 Shorts), ensuring clarity, consistency, and explicit acknowledgment of any missing data. Do not include tool references in the final output.

subtasks.description: Assemble per-ticker JSON objects with fields: ticker, position (long/short), thesis (1–2 sentences), key_drivers, key_risks, valuation_snapshot (FCF yield, EV/EBIT, vs peers/history), conviction (0–1, ≥0.5).
Quality control pass: verify schema compliance, numerical consistency with evidence, grammar/clarity, and inclusion of at least top 3 Longs and top 3 Shorts.
Explicitly state any material data gaps in the valuation_snapshot or risks if present, and reflect them in conviction levels. Avoid conjecture or unverifiable claims.
Deliver the final set of recommendations to the user. If unable to meet the minimum due to data gaps and low conviction, return 'No recommendations.'
